intro_script: Welcome back to your daily dose of robotics! It's July 8, 2026. Today we cover standardization in open-source pipelines, breakthroughs in end-to-end humanoid control, and who nailed the latest zero-shot AI benchmarks. Your essential tech update awaits.
outro_script: That's your robotics rundown for July 8th, feeling the power of open standards! Thanks for joining us. Go build something amazing with those new pipelines. Catch you tomorrow!

items.title: NVIDIA and Hugging Face Standardize Open-Source Robotics Pipeline via LeRobot
items.summary: NVIDIA has integrated its robotics tools with Hugging Face's platform through a standardized pipeline called LeRobot. This integration incorporates several key components from NVIDIA's suite. Specifically included are NVIDIA Isaac GR00T 1.7 and Isaac Teleop functionality. Furthermore, the upcoming Cosmos 3 world model will also be made available within this ecosystem. This collaboration standardizes open-source robotics development using these powerful tools on Hugging Face.
items.audio_script: From Humanoids Daily, "NVIDIA and Hugging Face Standardize Open-Source Robotics Pipeline via LeRobot." This development establishes a unified framework designed to streamline the open-source development process for robotic applications. The integration incorporates multiple specialized components from NVIDIA's extensive toolset directly into the widely used Hugging Face platform. Specifically, key elements being added include functionality derived from NVIDIA Isaac GR00T 1.7 and Isaac Teleop. Additionally, access is forthcoming for the Cosmos 3 world model within this new ecosystem. This collaboration effectively standardizes powerful tooling for the broader robotics community working with open-source methods. The industry can anticipate a more cohesive development environment for next-generation AI hardware integration.

items.title: Develop Humanoid Robot Policies End-to-End with NVIDIA Isaac GR00T
items.summary: Develops an end-to-end approach for creating policies for humanoid robots using NVIDIA Isaac GR00T. Addresses the growing need for repeatable workflows as teams advance beyond initial robot build-out. The platform facilitates task-specific skill development for these complex machines. This capability streamlines the process of programming and testing advanced robotic behaviors.
items.audio_script: From NVIDIA Developer Blog, "Develop Humanoid Robot Policies End-to-End with NVIDIA Isaac GR00T." This announcement details a comprehensive method for creating control policies for humanoid robots using the NVIDIA Isaac GR00T platform. The system addresses the increasing need for repeatable development workflows as teams move past initial hardware construction stages. Specifically, the platform facilitates task-specific skill development across complex mechanical systems, thereby streamlining both the programming and testing phases of advanced robotic behaviors. This marks a significant advancement in making sophisticated robotics more accessible to broader development groups.

items.title: Lumos Robotics tops global benchmark test for zero-shot embodied AI
items.summary: Lumos Robotics' Prime R0 industrial embodied AI model secured the top spot on the latest MolmoSpaces leaderboard. The Chinese company reported that its 2.8-billion-parameter model outperformed larger competitor models, including those from Nvidia. Its high performance was demonstrated across both single-arm fine manipulation and dual-arm collaboration tasks. Achieving this ranking solidifies Lumos Robotics' state-of-the-art capabilities in the field.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Lumos Robotics tops global benchmark test for zero-shot embodied AI." The report details how Lumos Robotics achieved the top position on the MolmoSpaces leaderboard with its industrial embodied AI model. The Chinese firm stated that its 2.8-billion-parameter Prime R0 model outperformed more substantial competitor models, including those developed by Nvidia. This superior performance was quantified across a range of functional evaluations, specifically in both single-arm fine manipulation and complex dual-arm collaborative tasks. Achieving this ranking represents a significant affirmation of Lumos Robotics' current capabilities within the field of embodied artificial intelligence. The results signal a competitive advancement in the commercial deployment benchmarks for advanced robotics platforms.

items.title: RLWRLD launches open platform to benchmark dexterous robotic hands
items.summary: RLWRLD, a physical AI company, launched "All Hands Up!" to benchmark dexterous robotic hands. This open web platform offers technical reports and visualization tools based on real-world operation data. The foundation for this work is RLWRLD's proprietary robotics model, RLDX-1. The platform aims to allow rigorous analysis of various commercially available dexterous robot hands.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "RLWRLD launches open platform to benchmark dexterous robotic hands." The story centers on a new web platform designed to provide an objective testing ground for advanced robotic manipulators. Details show that the physical AI company RLWRLD has introduced "All Hands Up!," an open resource providing technical reports and visualization tools derived from real-world operational data. This development is founded upon RLWRLD's internal robotics model, RLDX-1, establishing a framework for rigorous analysis across numerous commercially available dexterous robot hands. The platform represents a standardized means for the industry to assess current capabilities in advanced robotic manipulation hardware.

items.title: Niantic Spatial adds USDZ export to Scaniverse to streamline robotics simulation workflows
items.summary: Niantic Spatial has integrated USDZ export functionality into its Scaniverse application. This addition allows robotics developers to create simulation-ready digital twins from actual physical environments. The tool directly supports use with Nvidia Isaac Sim for advanced simulations. Its primary goal is to mitigate the persistent "sim-to-real" gap in robotics. By bridging this gap, it improves system performance when deploying trained models into complex real-world settings.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Niantic Spatial adds USDZ export to Scaniverse to streamline robotics simulation workflows." This development introduces a method for creating highly accurate digital representations of physical spaces for use in robotic testing environments. Niantic Spatial has integrated native USDZ export functionality within its Scaniverse platform. This capability permits robotics developers to generate simulation-ready digital twins directly from real-world scans, with specific support confirmed for integration into Nvidia Isaac Sim simulations. The core objective of this enhancement is the mitigation of the persistent sim-to-real gap in automated systems. Improved digital fidelity facilitates better system performance upon deployment into complex physical operational areas. This advancement signals a noticeable move toward greater interoperability across robotics simulation and real-world hardware deployment pipelines.

items.title: Opinion: Why robot dogs are leading the pack in factory floor efficiency
items.summary: Modern manufacturing facilities comprise vast, connected spaces requiring comprehensive oversight. Engineering teams struggle with maintaining clear visibility across diverse operational zones, including factory floors, plant rooms, and external assets. The increasing complexity of these sprawling environments demands better monitoring capabilities for continuous equipment operation. Robot dogs are being highlighted as a leading solution to address this need for broad site awareness. These robotic units provide crucial real-time intelligence across large physical footprints within industrial settings.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Opinion: Why robot dogs are leading the pack in factory floor efficiency." The report details how advanced robotic canines are emerging as a primary solution for improving oversight within expansive and complex modern manufacturing facilities. Engineering teams confront challenges maintaining clear visibility across diverse operational zones, including entire plant rooms and external assets, due to increasing environmental sprawl demanding better monitoring. These specific robotic units gather and transmit crucial real-time intelligence spanning large physical footprints throughout industrial settings, addressing comprehensive site awareness gaps. This development signals a growing industry trend toward utilizing mobile robotics for broad, distributed situational awareness within complex infrastructure environments.

items.title: Robust.AI selects Aptiv’s AI-powered perception system for next-generation Carter warehouse robot
items.summary: Robust.AI has chosen Aptiv's intelligent perception solutions for its next-generation Carter warehouse robot. These selected solutions include AI and Machine Learning based sensor fusion, which is powered by the Aptiv Pulse sensor. This integration will enhance the capabilities of Robust.AI's collaborative mobile robot. The decision follows an existing partnership between the two companies. The incorporation of Aptiv's proven technology marks a significant step in the robot's development.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Robust.AI selects Aptiv’s AI-powered perception system for next-generation Carter warehouse robot." This report details the selection of advanced perception technology by Robust.AI for an upcoming model of its autonomous material handling unit. The chosen solutions incorporate artificial intelligence and machine learning based sensor fusion, utilizing power provided by the Aptiv Pulse sensor to bolster the mobile robot's collaborative capabilities. This integration represents a notable advancement following a pre-existing operational partnership between the two technology firms. Industry observers anticipate this adoption of proven sensing technology will accelerate the deployment timeline for sophisticated warehouse automation solutions across logistics sectors.

items.title: ExRobotics launches UL-certified inspection robot for hazardous industrial sites in North America
items.summary: ExRobotics introduced its UL Certified ExR-2.5 autonomous inspection robot in North America. The launch occurred at the Energy Drone & Robotics Summit in Houston. Achieving this certification represents an industry first for the company. The ExR-2.5 is unique as the only UL Certified inspection robot designed for potentially explosive industrial environments. This provides operators with a recognized method for site inspections.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "ExRobotics launches UL-certified inspection robot for hazardous industrial sites in North America." The report details the introduction of a new autonomous inspection platform designed for high-risk operational areas. At the Energy Drone & Robotics Summit held in Houston, ExRobotics unveiled its product, the ExR-2.5 model. This device marks an industry first for the company through its receipt of UL certification status. The significance lies in the fact that the ExR-2.5 is reported to be the only such robot certified specifically for environments with potential explosive atmospheres, offering operators a recognized protocol for safety inspections. This development establishes new standards for autonomous technology deployment within critical infrastructure sectors.

items.title: ABB Robotics completes Visual SLAM AMR range with autonomous forklift launch
items.summary: ABB Robotics has expanded its Autonomous Mobile Robotics (AMR) line with the launch of the Flexley Stack F712. This new product establishes a fully interoperable ecosystem across all primary Visual SLAM AMR types offered by the company. The platform integrates autonomous forklifts, tugs, and movers into one unified system. This comprehensive capability allows customers to automate a wider variety of material-handling and intralogistics processes.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "ABB Robotics completes Visual SLAM AMR range with autonomous forklift launch." The report details ABB Robotics expanding its Autonomous Mobile Robotics line by introducing the Flexley Stack F712. This new product establishes a fully interoperable ecosystem encompassing all primary Visual SLAM AMR types provided by the company. Integration allows for the unification of autonomous forklifts, tugs, and movers into one cohesive system structure. This comprehensive capability permits customers to automate an expanded spectrum of material-handling and intralogistics processes within operational environments. The advancement reinforces industry trends toward integrated, modular automation solutions across diverse industrial settings.

items.title: Flytrex scales shared drone traffic system with thousands of coordinated flights
items.summary: Flytrex successfully scaled its shared airspace operations, managing thousands of coordinated drone flights monthly. This milestone demonstrates safe operation within complex aerial environments. The company achieved this feat using advanced technology for managed air traffic. It solidifies Flytrex's position as a leading commercial drone operator in the United States. No reported airspace conflicts were experienced during these high-volume operations.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "Flytrex scales shared drone traffic system with thousands of coordinated flights." The report details the successful scaling of shared airspace operations, demonstrating the management of thousands of coordinated drone flights on a monthly basis. This achievement highlights the safe operation capacity within complex aerial environments through the use of advanced technology for managed air traffic. Throughout these high-volume deployments, no reported airspace conflicts were experienced. This development solidifies Flytrex's standing as a key commercial drone operator within the United States market, setting a precedent for future automated aerial logistics networks.

items.title: A3’s Automate 2026 breaks records as demand for robotics, AI and automation grows
items.summary: Automate 2026, North America's largest robotics and automation event, achieved record attendance. Over 50,000 registrants and 1,230 exhibitors participated at McCormick Place in Chicago. The association for Advancing Automation (A3) hosted the successful event from June 22-25. The show covered cutting-edge technologies across robotics and automation sectors. It utilized a substantial 425,000 square feet of exhibition space.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "A3’s Automate 2026 breaks records as demand for robotics, AI and automation grows." The report details record levels of participation at North America's largest robotics and automation event. Over fifty thousand registrants attended the show, which utilized a significant four hundred twenty-five thousand square feet of exhibition space at McCormick Place in Chicago. One thousand two hundred thirty exhibitors also participated in the successful event hosted by the Association for Advancing Automation from June twenty-second through the twenty-fifth, covering cutting-edge technologies across multiple automation sectors. This sustained high level of industry engagement indicates continued robust market adoption of advanced robotics and artificial intelligence solutions.

items.title: HelloFresh expands chilled warehouse capacity fivefold with Locus Robotics automation
items.summary: HelloFresh has significantly expanded its refrigerated fulfillment capabilities by incorporating Locus Robotics' automated cold-storage hardware. This modification increases their chilled SKU capacity from 100 to 500. The fivefold increase supports both greater meal variety offerings. Furthermore, this expansion unlocks new revenue opportunities across the entire HelloFresh brand portfolio. One of the brands benefiting is Factor.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "HelloFresh expands chilled warehouse capacity fivefold with Locus Robotics automation." The report details HelloFresh's substantial upgrade to its refrigerated fulfillment infrastructure. This expansion was achieved through the integration of automated cold-storage hardware provided by Locus Robotics. The operational enhancement directly increases the company's chilled stock keeping unit capacity from one hundred units to five hundred units. This significant fivefold increase is intended to support a broader range of available meal options, and it simultaneously unlocks new revenue streams across the entire HelloFresh brand group, notably benefiting Factor. Industry observers note this represents a growing trend where advanced warehouse automation supports multi-brand food service scalability.

items.title: South Korean robotic hand developer Tesollo begins IPO preparations after Series B funding
items.summary: South Korean robotic hand developer Tesollo has initiated preparations for an initial public offering (IPO). The IPO will be through Korea's technology-special listing track, with a planned listing following 2027. In March, the company appointed KB Securities to serve as its lead underwriter. Furthermore, Tesollo recently finalized its Series B funding round.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "South Korean robotic hand developer Tesollo begins IPO preparations after Series B funding." The report details that Tesollo has commenced planning for an initial public offering aimed at the technology-special listing track in South Korea, with a potential listing scheduled for after 2027. In connection with these developments, the company formally appointed KB Securities to take on the role of lead underwriter. Moreover, Tesollo recently completed its Series B funding round, solidifying its financial backing ahead of the public offering process. This strategic move signals increased market confidence in specialized automation hardware and provides an indicator of continued growth within advanced robotics sectors.

items.title: AIxCrypto launches RoboShare robot rental platform and autonomous asset network
items.summary: AIxCrypto launched RoboShare, a robot rental marketplace, alongside a new infrastructure platform for autonomous assets. The company introduced these tools at the Automate Show in Chicago. This initiative aims to extend the commercial utility of robots beyond their initial purchase. RoboShare facilitates the renting of various robotic units. Furthermore, it establishes an autonomous asset network to support diverse AI agents and machinery.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "AIxCrypto launches RoboShare robot rental platform and autonomous asset network." The company introduced a new system designed to expand the commercial application scope of robotic technology through leasing models and shared infrastructure access. At the Automate Show in Chicago, AIxCrypto unveiled two main initiatives: RoboShare, which operates as a dedicated marketplace for renting various types of robotic units, and a complementary infrastructure platform established specifically to support a burgeoning network of autonomous assets utilized by diverse artificial intelligence agents and mechanical machinery. This development signals a shift toward utility-based access for robotics, potentially lowering the barrier to entry for businesses seeking advanced automation capabilities.

items.title: IEEE Honors Robotics Pioneer Toshio Fukuda
items.summary: IEEE recognized robotic pioneer Toshio Fukuda with its Richard M. Emberson Award for advancing robotics. His extensive contributions include developing biomedical and industrial robotic systems, micro-nano robotics, and mechatronics. Fukuda pioneered modular, cellular robotic systems (CEBOTs), allowing them to assemble complex structures like interlocking bricks. He also helped develop brachiation robots mimicking monkey movement for inspecting infrastructure. A highly prolific scholar, he served as IEEE president in 2020 and is currently affiliated with the Egypt-Japan University of Science and Technology.
items.audio_script: From IEEE Spectrum - Robotics, "IEEE Honors Robotics Pioneer Toshio Fukuda." The publication reports on the recognition of robotic pioneer Toshio Fukuda with the Richard M. Emberson Award for substantial advancements in the field of robotics. Fukuda’s work encompasses numerous areas, including the development of biomedical and industrial robotic systems, advances in micro-nano robotics, and mechatronics applications. A notable contribution was the pioneering of modular, cellular robotic systems, known as CEBOTs, which demonstrated the capability to self-assemble complex structures resembling interlocking bricks. Furthermore, his research involved creating brachiation robots designed to mimic primate movement for infrastructure inspection purposes. The award acknowledges his prolific scholarship and his service as IEEE president in 2020; Fukuda remains affiliated with the Egypt-Japan University of Science and Technology. This recognition highlights sustained foundational contributions essential for the future development trajectory of advanced robotic systems across multiple industries.

items.title: #RoboCup2026 social media round-up
items.summary: RoboCup took place in Incheon, South Korea, from July 2nd to July 6th. Participants engaged in various activities including competitions, training sessions, and a symposium. The social media round-up documented the actions of the event's participants. Furthermore, the content announced the official start date for RoboCup 2026.
items.audio_script: From Robohub, "#RoboCup2026 social media round-up" documents the recent event and confirms the start date for the following year's competition. The RoboCup took place in Incheon, South Korea, spanning from July second through July sixth, where participants engaged across multiple facets of the event itinerary. Activities reported included rigorous competitions alongside structured training sessions and a dedicated symposium addressing technological advancements. This documentation compiled various actions undertaken by the attendees throughout the specified timeframe. The content also officially announced the commencement date for RoboCup 2026. Continued interest in autonomous robotics is highlighted, suggesting sustained development activity within the artificial intelligence sector.

items.title: Quaternion-Averaging-Based Adaptive Complementary Filter for Pedestrian Dead Reckoning With a Foot-Mounted AHRS
items.summary: Pedestrian Dead Reckoning offers an indoor navigation solution unaffected by GPS signal degradation. The accuracy of foot-mounted AHRS-based PDR heavily relies on the employed attitude estimation algorithm. A Quaternion-Averaging-Based Adaptive Complementary Filter (QAACF) is proposed to improve this accuracy while lowering computational demands. QAACF integrates quaternions from angular velocity with those from acceleration and magnetic field using Markley's quaternion averaging. The method further adaptively adjusts sensor weights based on gait phases and magnetic disturbances, showing lower RMSEs than existing filters with reduced computational overhead compared to Kalman filters.

items.title: Learning 4D Geometric Priors for Inference-Efficient World Action Models
items.summary: World Action Models (WAMs) for robotics often fail to capture necessary temporal geometry using appearance-based video latents. MECo-WAM introduces a Multi-Expert Co-Training approach by injecting action-relevant 4D geometric priors into video-action representations. During training, the model uses a lightweight 4D expert supervised by relational targets from a frozen encoder and employs asymmetric visibility to prevent spurious shortcuts. A decayed 4D read-mask attention progressively transfers this geometric knowledge while minimizing deployment cost. Evaluation on datasets like LIBERO and RoboTwin confirms that MECo-WAM improves manipulation performance significantly without increasing inference complexity.

items.title: GEM-Occ: From Visual Geometry Evidence to Embodied Semantic Occupancy Memory
items.summary: Semantic occupancy memory is crucial for embodied agents navigating connected indoor spaces by structuring representations of occupied regions, free space, unknown areas, and object semantics. Recognizing gaps in current research focusing on single-view or room-level perception, the authors introduce HIOcc, a benchmark unifying multiple indoor datasets into a consistent sparse semantic format supporting local, room-level, and building-level evaluation. They propose GEM-Occ, a Gaussian Evidence Memory framework that treats local visual predictions as transient evidence rather than fixed pointmaps. GEM-Occ converts this evidence into semantic Gaussian occupancy and free-space ray data for persistent storage via causal updates. This hierarchical memory structure—comprising caches, submaps, and a building graph—improves performance across all tested aspects compared to prior baselines on the HIOcc benchmark.

items.title: Dynamic Evaluation of Classical and Control-Aware Optimal Trajectory Planning in Robot Manipulators
items.summary: Trajectory planning significantly impacts robot manipulator performance metrics like tracking accuracy and actuator demand. Classical planners are purely kinematic, leading to inefficiencies when executing nominally smooth paths due to ignored system dynamics. This paper introduces a control-aware optimal trajectory planning framework incorporating explicit manipulator dynamics and actuator effort into a finite-horizon formulation. A unique unified evaluation framework tests all planners under identical closed-loop nonlinear execution conditions. Simulations on an UR5 manipulator demonstrate that the proposed method consistently reduces tracking error, corrective torque, and overall execution cost compared to classical approaches.

items.title: Physics-Regularized Machine Learning for Proprioceptive Vehicle Localization Using Onboard Sensors
items.summary: Accurate vehicle localization is critical for autonomous systems, but performance degrades during sensor outages despite IMU/satellite fusion. This paper introduces Physics-Regularized Machine Learning for Localization (PRML2), a hybrid framework combining Kalman filtering and data-driven learning. PRML2 leverages physics-regularization by training an ML model through a differentiable Kalman filter, ensuring consistency with vehicle motion models. This approach enhances localization accuracy and generalization across various driving conditions. Testing shows PRML2 achieves superior, real-time localization performance using only onboard sensors and introduces a novel dataset for low-friction research.

items.title: Efficient Transfer Learning of Robot Dynamic Models Using Morphological Similarity
items.summary: A new neural network framework proposes transfer learning to model soft, fin-actuated underwater robot dynamics. This approach specializes in handling morphologically similar robots that vary only in scale and hydrodynamic traits. The method utilizes an autoencoder-based domain adaptation technique to create a shared latent representation of the robot dynamics. By aligning this representation, it facilitates efficient knowledge transfer from a larger source robot to a smaller target robot. Testing on two real underwater robots confirmed accurate state estimation on the target platform without requiring labeled data for fine-tuning.

items.title: Uncertainty-Aware Velocity Correction for Proprioceptive Vehicle Localization using Evidential Mamba
items.summary: Addressing reliable localization challenges in GPS-denied environments, the authors propose Evidential Velocity Correction using Mamba (EVC-Mamba). This novel, learning-based architecture estimates a virtual velocity sensor directly from onboard vehicle data, eliminating the need for extra hardware. It combines a Mamba selective state space model to capture motion dynamics with evidential deep learning for principled uncertainty quantification. The resultant uncertainty-aware velocity estimate corrects IMU drift when fed into an Error-State Extended Kalman Filter. Testing on real data showed this method achieved localization accuracy within 10% of dedicated external sensors, supporting real-time operation on edge hardware during extended GPS outages.

items.title: IMR: Iterative Mode-World Weighted Regression for Multi-Agent Trajectory Prediction
items.summary: Multi-agent motion prediction is crucial for safe automated vehicle operation, as previous methods have limitations in mode diversity or prediction accuracy. To overcome these shortcomings, a mode-world weighted regression loss is introduced to improve both world ranking and top-1 confidence while mitigating mode collapse. The proposed framework also incorporates an iterative decoder to enhance prediction accuracy through recurrent and segmental trajectory generation. Empirical testing demonstrates that this novel approach achieves the highest ranking on the Argoverse 2 multi-agent motion forecasting benchmark.

items.title: Co-STAR: Cognitive Stimulation Therapy by an Autonomous Robot for Dementia -- A One-Week In-Home Study
items.summary: Developed and deployed an autonomous social robot capable of providing Cognitive Stimulation Therapy (CST) at home to address shortages of trained professionals. Nine people living with dementia participated in a one-week study involving daily robot-led sessions. Participants engaged positively, achieving an adherence rate higher than typical rates observed during caregiver-led CST. Family members played a supportive role by initiating sessions and joining activities. This research demonstrates the feasibility and potential for using socially assistive robots to offer scalable in-home cognitive therapy for dementia care.

items.title: Observation Quality Matters: Robust Multi-Fisheye Calibration via Failure-Oriented Analysis
items.summary: Calibrating multi-fisheye camera systems is challenging due to increasing complexity and reliance on high-quality observations. Research reveals that calibration failures are primarily caused by intrinsic initialization issues when limited radial span data couples focal scale with fisheye projection parameters, leading to ill-conditioned updates. The paper proposes CO-Calib, a framework integrating a learning-based target detector and an error-analysis-guided frame selector. CO-Calib creates initialization-friendly anchors and multi-camera constraints without altering existing calibration workflows. Experiments show CO-Calib significantly boosts success rates from 68.1% to 99.3% while improving accuracy on various systems.
